Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search emails for context on the Kevin Tran keyboard receipt and the duplicate receipt audit. Tool: search_emails with query "Kevin Tran" or "duplicate" or "receipt" or "TechComfort". Finds Marcus's email to Mina (from marcus.thorne@moveops.com, cc dmitri.volkov@moveops.com) reporting Dmitri's audit discovery - Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard receipt from TechComfort Supply is a byte-for-byte identical PDF to Andre Washington's submission at Mosaic Robotics. Kevin's $89 was already paid out (STIP-2026-0089, March 8). Marcus asks Mina to verify with Rachel Whitfield at Vectral. Also finds Mina's verification email to Rachel Whitfield (rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com) asking whether Vectral has documentation of Kevin purchasing the keyboard and whether Kevin was reimbursed through Vectral's own system.
- Search emails for Rachel Whitfield's communications with Mina to check if a verification reply exists. Tool: search_emails with query "Rachel Whitfield" or "Vectral" or "Kevin Tran". Finds Rachel's renewal demand email (from rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) citing three service failures, including "Kevin Tran's stipend receipt flagged in fraud audit that turned out to be legitimate" - Rachel's position is the receipt was legitimate and the audit caused unnecessary friction. Also finds Rachel's reply to the verification request (email_rachel_kevin_tran_fraud_reply) confirming that Kevin did purchase the keyboard from TechComfort Supply but was already reimbursed through Vectral's internal expense system on March 3 — Kevin says he never submitted anything through MoveOps and did not know a claim existed under his name. The MoveOps submission was unauthorized. Also finds Mina's forwarding email to Samira (email_mina_fraud_escalation_samira) relaying this finding and asking engineering to investigate the access control question — this is the inciting event of the prompt.
- Search emails for David Chen's internal communications about the Vectral renewal to discover the contradiction with the prompt's inciting event. Tool: search_emails with query "Vectral" or "renewal" or "discount" or "Rachel Whitfield". Finds David Chen's email to Marcus and Alejandro stating that Rachel cited "the Kevin Tran stipend receipt that was flagged in the fraud audit and turned out to be legitimate" as one of the service failures. This directly contradicts the prompt's new information that Kevin says he never submitted the receipt.
- Search Slack #engineering (C003) for Dmitri's detection work updates on ENG-220. Tool: conversations_search_messages with query "duplicate detection" or "ENG-220" or "hash" or "audit" in channel C003. Finds Dmitri's message (moveops_dmitri_volkov, ts 1775598300, April 7) titled "Audit Finding on Stipends - Detection of Duplicate Receipts" reporting: Case A - Kevin Tran (Vectral, $89) and Andre Washington (Mosaic, $89) have byte-for-byte identical PDFs (same SHA-256 hash a3f7c8d1e9b2...f0a2b4), same vendor TechComfort Supply, same PDF template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041. Case B - Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop, $45) has a different hash but identical PDF /Creator metadata (TechComfort Supply Invoice Generator v2.1) and the same template ID. Also finds Dmitri's follow-up (ts 1775604480) reporting he filed ENG-220 with full specifications and will begin Layer 1 hash dedup implementation on Monday. Additionally finds Dmitri's more recent update (ts 1777032000, April 23) reporting the PDF metadata pattern is tighter than initially thought — all three use the same PDF generator (TechComfort Supply Invoice Generator v2.1) and same template ID (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041), with the Kevin Tran and Andre Washington receipts being byte-for-byte identical. Dmitri asks Samira for direction on whether to keep building the detection pipeline or pivot to investigating the existing submissions as potential fraud.
- Retrieve the ENG-220 ticket (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) and its comments to understand the full detection system design and the original flagged transactions. Tool: linear_get_issue on issue_stipend_duplicate_detection then linear_list_comments. Finds the full ticket description with the three flagged transactions table, financial exposure ($223 total, $89 paid, $134 pending), and the DuplicateDetector pipeline proposal. Also finds Dmitri's comments: comment_dmitri_duplicate_discovery (detailed forensic findings with hash comparisons and PDF metadata analysis) and comment_dmitri_duplicate_ticket_context (linking Airtable records, QuickBooks holds, and AM verification requests sent to Vectral, Mosaic, and BrightLoop).
- Retrieve the ENG-216 ticket (issue_audit_expenses) to understand the scope of the original audit. Tool: linear_get_issue on issue_audit_expenses. Finds the ticket description: full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses across Vectral and Mosaic, assigned to Dmitri Volkov. The comprehensive audit results (5 false rejections for Vectral, 3 false approvals for Mosaic, and the duplicate receipt discovery that led to ENG-220) are documented in comment_dmitri_audit_results on issue_pipeline_hardening.
- Search Slack #finance (C005) for discussions about the financial status of the flagged stipends. Tool: conversations_search_messages with query "stipend" or "hold" or "STIP" in channel C005. Finds Marcus's message (moveops_marcus_thorne, ts 1775597280, April 7) reporting admin holds placed on two pending stipend reimbursements: STIP-2026-0094 (Andre Washington, Mosaic, $89, duplicate receipt hash) and STIP-2026-0097 (Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop, $45, matched PDF template). Notes that Kevin Tran's $89 (Vectral) was already disbursed on 3/8. Total on hold: $134. Total potential loss if all three fraudulent: $223. Also finds Marcus's more recent message (ts 1777100400, April 24) addressed to Samira, requesting engineering input on the stipend holds before finance can close it out — reiterating the $223 total exposure with $89 already disbursed and asking whether this is a system issue or something else.
- Search Slack #finance (C005) for Hana Kim's reconciliation tracking. Tool: conversations_search_messages with query "TechComfort" or "reconciliation" or "STIP" in channel C005. Finds Hana's message (moveops_hana_kim, ts 1775599920, April 7) reporting she is marking all three stipends (STIP-2026-0089, 0094, 0097) as under investigation in the reconciliation tracker and will verify the TechComfort Supply vendor name against other stipend submissions in the ledger to check for any overlooked transactions. Also finds Hana's follow-up reply (ts 1777105800, April 24) confirming that TechComfort Supply does not exist as a registered vendor in QuickBooks — no purchase orders, no vendor onboarding documentation, and no prior transactions. The three stipend bills reference client companies as VendorRef, not TechComfort itself, meaning the receipts reference a vendor MoveOps has no business relationship with.
- Search emails for the verification requests sent to client HR representatives for the other two flagged receipts. Tool: search_emails with query "Andre Washington" or "Jordan Ekwueme" or "Tamara" or "Tessa" or "verification". Finds Emeka's email to Tamara Byrd (tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com) about Andre Washington's $89 keyboard receipt being a byte-for-byte match to another client's submission, asking Mosaic to verify. Also finds Emeka's email to Tessa Moreno (tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com) about Jordan Ekwueme's $45 monitor stand receipt sharing PDF metadata with other flagged receipts, asking BrightLoop to verify. No email replies from Tamara or Tessa are found in the email data. However, the verification results are available via Emeka's Slack update in #customer-engagement (see next OE).
- Search Slack #customer-engagement (C002) for AM verification updates on the flagged receipts. Tool: conversations_search_messages with query "stipend" or "verification" or "Andre Washington" or "Jordan Ekwueme" in channel C002. Finds Emeka's message (moveops_emeka_diallo, ts 1777110000, April 24) reporting the verification results from both clients: Tamara Byrd at Mosaic says Mosaic does not maintain their own internal expense reimbursement system for relocating employees and relies entirely on MoveOps, so she cannot independently verify whether Andre actually purchased the keyboard — the Andre Washington receipt is unverifiable. Tessa Moreno at BrightLoop confirmed that Jordan Ekwueme did purchase a monitor stand and has been asking when he would be reimbursed — he submitted the receipt through MoveOps directly via personal email instead of going through BrightLoop HR, so the Jordan submission appears legitimate but used a nonstandard submission method. Emeka's summary: Kevin Tran is confirmed unauthorized by Vectral, Andre Washington is unverifiable because Mosaic has no parallel system, Jordan Ekwueme appears legitimate but nonstandard — three different situations, not one pattern. Also finds David Chen's reply (ts 1777113600) instructing not to share the fraud angle with any clients until engineering and finance have aligned on messaging.
- Search or list Airtable stipend records for the three flagged transactions. Tool: airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on tblStipends00001 with queries for Kevin Tran, Andre Washington, Jordan Ekwueme. Finds rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard ($89, Approved, Audit Flag: Duplicate Hash, same SHA-256 as Andre Washington), rec_stipend_andre_keyboard ($89, Pending Approval, Audit Flag: Duplicate Hash), rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand ($45, Pending Approval, Audit Flag: PDF Template Match). All three reference TechComfort Supply as the vendor with the matching PDF template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041.
- Look up Mina Hashimoto's contact details for the reply email. Tool: contacts_search_contacts with query "Mina Hashimoto". Finds mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
- Update the ENG-220 ticket (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) to reflect the expanded scope from duplicate detection to submission integrity and fraud investigation. Tool: linear_update_issue on issue_stipend_duplicate_detection with updated description or priority reflecting the escalation.
- Create a new Linear issue for the engineering incident tracking the access control gap in the ExpenseBot submission pipeline. Tool: linear_create_issue with team team_engineering, title referencing the access control or unauthorized submission investigation, priority Urgent or P1.
- Send an email to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com from samira.tariq@moveops.com with engineering's response to the fraud escalation, covering what engineering found, what tickets have been created or updated, and what Mina can communicate to Rachel Whitfield. Tool: send_email.
- Post a message in #engineering (C003) announcing the priority escalation, the expanded scope of ENG-220, the new incident ticket, and direction for the engineering team. Tool: conversations_add_message with channel_id C003.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The ENG-220 ticket (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) was updated (via linear_update_issue) to reflect the expanded scope beyond duplicate detection.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a `linear_update_issue` call targeting `issue_stipend_duplicate_detection`, and the tool result confirms success (updated_at timestamp changed to 2026-04-08T13:27:48Z). The updated description clearly expands the scope from duplicate detection to fraud prevention.

The agent apparently could not find Emeka's Slack messages about client verifications.
